title: Allow Admins to Proxy as different
excerpt: We use Monday.com as a marketing agency. We use this to track not just the client work we do but also to keep track of all of our internal processes including more private information such as hiring progress and other HR…

posts.author: jsmith91 (Jarod Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: We use Monday.com as a marketing agency. We use this to track not just the client work we do but also to keep track of all of our internal processes including more private information such as hiring progress and other HR related things. When we are setting up these workspaces, we are often asking other members of the team who we know are not admin to report back to us what things look like on their end to ensure the set up that we do is appropriate to the work. We would love to see a way for admins to proxy in as another user or even as another permissions level so we can more cleanly and quickly ensure the private information stays private and all public information is truly public.

posts.author: TinaM (Tina Montalto)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
Not sure if there is another similar proxy question, but posting this here. As an admin and key user it would be helpful to be able to proxy as another user to ensure access/permissions are set up correctly. I typically have to arrange calls or ask for screenshots (separate email confirmations) with/from users to ensure they really only see what they are supposed to see. We ran into some access related issues in the past, so proxy options e.g. limited proxy seats to 2-3 admins. would be tremendously helpful.
Thanks.

posts.author: DrGerry (Gerald)
posts.cookedHtml: Coming from ServiceNow and Moodle, as an Admin I am used to being able to “log in as” another User so I can see exactly what they see. Super helpful when analyzing problems. Often, problems are not really problems, just user error.

posts.author: AntoineHebert (AntoineHebert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Having the ability for admins to slide into someone else’s shoes for a quick peek would be a game-changer. That way, we can ensure everything’s shipshape without risking anyone’s privacy.
Imagine this: you’re an admin, but you want to see how the setup looks for a regular team member or even a different permission level. Boom! A proxy feature would make it happen in a snap. It’d be like having a secret identity, but for admin tasks – pretty neat, huh?
Oh, and speaking of solutions, I recently stumbled upon Proxy solutions, and they seem like they could be a real game-changer for this kind of thing.

posts.author: ebannister (Elliot Bannister)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this idea. Moodle and Drupal have this feature. On Monday.com, it’s very hard to keep track of what other users are seeing and able to do.

posts.author: victoria.lennon (Victoria Lennon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, this would be a useful feature to have, especially as a new admin navigating Monday. It allows for much better testing of a board to make sure it is working before releasing.

posts.author: Rivka (Rivka McCormack)
posts.cookedHtml:
yeah there are two versions of this really.

A “View as Member/Viewer/Guest” setting to let us look at big picture set up and permissions as other user types. super helpful when setting up a workspace.

and a “View as fill in user’s name here” which would help you focus on that individuals setting and permissions. This would be amazing for both ADMIN set up AND as a IT help desk type situation, where someone cant sort out why something is going on. While this sometimes can be handled by screen sharing, that comes with a whole other set of struggles and is not for asynchronous problem solving or testing.

posts.author: cphu (Christine Phu)
posts.cookedHtml: This would help us solve problems soo much faster.
